The commenting backend is pluggable. The SDK currently bundles
integration. You can connect any commenting backend by implementing

inlineContent.text: That’s it. The SDK will show a comment button at the bottom of eligible articles,
display comment counts in the content browser, and prefetch counts at edition

For full control over every aspect of the commenting experience, implement
directly. This is an advanced pattern — you take on
responsibility for the data layer, button creation, tap handling, and tooltips:

code: // Handle .preferCache / .preferNetwork as needed for your backend
fetchCommentCount(forArticle: article, inEdition: source, completion: completion)
// Return your custom button, or nil to suppress
